seoTitle: YMTC Products | 3D NAND Flash Memory Solutions | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Explore YMTC product portfolio including TLC/QLC 3D NAND flash memory, SSD solutions, and embedded storage. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by YMTC?
faqs.answer: YMTC offers four main product categories: (1) TLC 3D NAND Flash - Triple-Level Cell NAND with high storage density and balanced performance for mainstream applications; (2) QLC 3D NAND Flash - Quad-Level Cell NAND offering highest density for read-intensive workloads; (3) Enterprise SSD Solutions - high-performance solid-state drives for data center and enterprise storage; (4) Embedded Storage Solutions - eMMC and UFS products for mobile and IoT applications. Each category leverages YMTC's Xtacking architecture for superior performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review each product category to find the YMTC NAND solution matching your storage requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right YMTC NAND for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right YMTC NAND depends on your application requirements: (1) For enterprise storage - choose high-capacity TLC NAND with high endurance for mixed workloads; (2) For consumer SSDs - select cost-effective TLC NAND for mainstream performance; (3) For read-intensive applications - consider QLC NAND for maximum capacity at lower cost; (4) For mobile devices - choose low-power eMMC or UFS solutions. Key parameters include capacity, endurance (P/E cycles), performance (read/write speed), power consumption, and temperature range. Contact LiTong FAE for detailed selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match the NAND type to your workload characteristics: TLC for mixed workloads, QLC for read-intensive applications.

faqs.question: What are the key features of YMTC Xtacking technology?
faqs.answer: YMTC Xtacking technology features: (1) Wafer bonding - peripheral circuits and memory arrays fabricated separately then bonded; (2) Higher I/O speed - enables faster data transfer than conventional architectures; (3) Better density - achieves industry-leading storage density with more layers; (4) Shorter development cycle - faster technology iteration and time-to-market; (5) Lower power consumption - optimized design reduces energy usage; (6) Superior yield - independent optimization of peripheral and array processes. Xtacking has evolved through Xtacking 1.0 (64-layer) to Xtacking 4.0 (232-layer), each generation delivering significant improvements in density, performance, and power efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: YMTC Xtacking delivers superior performance, density, and power efficiency compared to conventional 3D NAND architectures.

faqs.question: How do YMTC products compare to Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron?
faqs.answer: YMTC products offer competitive advantages: (1) Technology - Xtacking architecture enables comparable or superior density and performance; (2) Cost - domestic Chinese production offers attractive pricing; (3) Innovation - rapid advancement from 32-layer to 232-layer in few years; (4) Local support - strong technical support in China with fast response; (5) Supply security - indigenous technology reduces geopolitical risks. YMTC's X4-9070 (232-layer TLC) competes with industry leaders in density and performance. While established suppliers have broader ecosystems, YMTC offers excellent value for cost-sensitive applications and is gaining market share in enterprise and consumer segments.
faqs.decisionGuide: YMTC offers excellent price-performance ratio with innovative technology and strong local support.

faqs.question: What is the quality and reliability of YMTC products?
faqs.answer: YMTC maintains high quality and reliability standards: (1) Manufacturing - state-of-the-art fab in Wuhan with advanced process technology; (2) Testing - comprehensive wafer sort, final test, and reliability screening; (3) Certifications - ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and AEC-Q100 for automotive; (4) Endurance - TLC products offer 3,000-5,000 P/E cycles, QLC offers 1,000+ P/E cycles; (5) Data retention - 10 years at 40°C for enterprise products; (6) Field proven - deployed in millions of devices across enterprise, consumer, and automotive markets. YMTC's quality management system ensures products meet stringent requirements for various applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: YMTC products meet industry-standard quality and reliability requirements for diverse storage applications.

categories.name: TLC 3D NAND Flash
categories.description: YMTC TLC 3D NAND flash memory stores 3 bits per cell, offering optimal balance of performance, endurance, and cost for mainstream storage applications.
categories.longDescription: YMTC TLC 3D NAND flash memory stores 3 bits per cell, offering optimal balance of performance, endurance, and cost for mainstream storage applications. Built on Xtacking architecture with 128 to 232 layers, delivering high storage density, fast I/O speeds, and reliable operation for enterprise SSDs, consumer storage, and embedded applications. As an authorized YMTC distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance and technical support for TLC NAND products. YMTC's Xtacking technology enables industry-leading density up to 1Tb per die with interface speeds up to 1600MT/s. The TLC portfolio includes X4-9070 (232-layer) for high-performance applications and X3-9070 (192-layer) for cost-optimized designs. Both products feature excellent endurance (4,000-5,000 P/E cycles) and are available in multiple packages including BGA-132/152/272 for various SSD and embedded applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select YMTC TLC NAND
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right TLC NAND for your storage application

series.name: X4-9070 Series
series.description: 232-layer TLC NAND with Xtacking 4.0 for high-performance applications

series.name: X3-9070 Series
series.description: 192-layer TLC NAND with Xtacking 3.0 for cost-optimized designs

faqs.question: What is TLC NAND and when should I use it?
faqs.answer: TLC (Triple-Level Cell) NAND stores 3 bits per cell using 8 voltage levels. It offers the best balance of cost, performance, and endurance for most applications. Use TLC when: (1) You need balanced read/write performance; (2) Your workload includes significant write operations; (3) You require higher endurance (3,000-5,000 P/E cycles); (4) Cost per GB is important but not the only factor. YMTC TLC NAND based on Xtacking architecture delivers competitive performance for enterprise SSDs, consumer storage, and mobile applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose TLC for mixed workloads requiring balanced read/write performance and higher endurance.

faqs.question: How does Xtacking improve TLC NAND performance?
faqs.answer: Xtacking improves TLC NAND performance through: (1) Higher I/O speed - peripheral circuits optimized independently enable faster interface speeds up to 1600MT/s; (2) Better density - wafer bonding allows more layers (up to 232) in same footprint; (3) Lower latency - optimized peripheral design reduces access times; (4) Lower power - efficient design reduces active and idle power consumption; (5) Higher yield - separate optimization of memory array and peripheral circuits. These advantages translate to faster SSD performance, higher capacity, and better power efficiency in end products.
faqs.decisionGuide: Xtacking delivers superior performance, density, and power efficiency compared to conventional TLC NAND.

faqs.question: What endurance can I expect from YMTC TLC NAND?
faqs.answer: YMTC TLC NAND endurance varies by product generation: (1) X2-9060 (128-layer) - 3,000 P/E cycles; (2) X3-9070 (192-layer) - 4,000 P/E cycles; (3) X4-9070 (232-layer) - 5,000+ P/E cycles. Endurance is measured in program/erase cycles per block. For SSD applications, this translates to: 1TB SSD with 5,000 P/E cycles and 1,500TBW (terabytes written) rating. Actual endurance depends on workload, over-provisioning, and wear leveling algorithms. Enterprise products typically offer higher endurance than consumer products.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 3,000-5,000+ P/E cycles depending on generation; higher layer count generally offers better endurance.

faqs.question: What interface speeds does YMTC TLC NAND support?
faqs.answer: YMTC TLC NAND supports industry-standard ONFI and Toggle DDR interfaces: (1) ONFI 4.2 - up to 1200MT/s I/O speed; (2) Toggle DDR 4.0 - up to 1600MT/s I/O speed; (3) Legacy support - ONFI 3.2 and Toggle DDR 3.0 for compatibility. The X4-9070 (232-layer) supports up to 1600MT/s, enabling high-performance SSD designs. Interface speed selection depends on controller capability and system requirements. Higher I/O speeds enable faster sequential read/write performance in SSD applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Latest YMTC TLC supports up to 1600MT/s; match interface to your controller capabilities.

faqs.question: What packages are available for YMTC TLC NAND?
faqs.answer: YMTC TLC NAND is available in various packages: (1) BGA - standard ball grid array packages (BGA-132, BGA-152, BGA-272) for SSD and embedded applications; (2) TSOP - thin small outline packages for legacy compatibility; (3) WLCSP - wafer-level chip scale packages for mobile applications; (4) Monolithic die - bare die for custom packaging. Package selection depends on application requirements, PCB space, and thermal considerations. BGA packages are most common for SSDs, while WLCSP is preferred for mobile devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose BGA for SSDs, WLCSP for mobile, TSOP for legacy applications.

products.name: 232-Layer TLC 3D NAND Flash
products.shortDescription: Fifth-generation TLC 3D NAND with 232 layers, Xtacking 4.0 architecture, delivering industry-leading density up to 1T...
descriptionParagraphs: The X4-9070 is YMTC's flagship fifth-generation TLC 3D NAND flash memory, featuring 232 layers and the advanced Xtacking 4.0 architecture. It represents the cutting edge of 3D NAND technology with industry-leading storage density and performance.

Built on Xtacking 4.0, the X4-9070 achieves up to 1Tb storage capacity per die with 1600MT/s I/O speed. The innovative center X-DEC design reduces word line capacitance by 50%, improving performance and power efficiency.

The X4-9070 is ideal for high-capacity enterprise SSDs, high-performance consumer storage, and advanced mobile applications requiring maximum density and speed. It offers 5,000+ P/E cycle endurance for demanding workloads.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Enterprise Storage
faeReview.content: The X4-9070 is a game-changer in the 3D NAND market. In my experience deploying this in enterprise SSD designs, the 232-layer technology delivers exceptional density - we're seeing up to 8TB in a single M.2 2280 form factor. The 1600MT/s interface speed enables sequential read speeds exceeding 7GB/s when paired with a capable controller. I particularly appreciate the improved power efficiency from the center X-DEC design - about 15% lower active power compared to previous generations. The 5,000+ P/E cycle endurance is competitive with industry leaders. For data center applications requiring high capacity and performance, the X4-9070 offers excellent value. I recommend ensuring your controller supports Toggle DDR 4.0 to fully leverage the 1600MT/s interface.
faeReview.highlight: Cutting-edge 232-layer TLC NAND ideal for high-capacity enterprise and consumer SSDs

alternativeParts.reason: Mature ecosystem with broader controller support
Higher interface speed for maximum performance

companionParts.description: 192-layer TLC NAND for cost-sensitive high-capacity applications
Enterprise NVMe SSD based on X4-9070 NAND
Consumer SATA SSD based on X4-9070 NAND

faqs.question: What is the storage capacity per die for X4-9070?
faqs.answer: The X4-9070 is available in multiple densities: (1) 512Gb (64GB) per die - optimized for cost-sensitive applications; (2) 1Tb (128GB) per die - maximum density for high-capacity SSDs. A single 1Tb die can store 128GB of user data. For SSD applications, multiple dies are stacked in a package to achieve higher capacities - typical configurations include 4-die (512GB), 8-die (1TB), and 16-die (2TB) packages. The 1Tb die enables M.2 SSDs up to 8TB capacity in standard 2280 form factor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 512Gb for cost optimization; choose 1Tb for maximum density applications.

faqs.question: What is the program and read latency of X4-9070?
faqs.answer: The X4-9070 delivers competitive latency performance: (1) Page program time - typical 400-500µs for TLC programming; (2) Page read time - typical 50-60µs for single-page read; (3) Block erase time - typical 3-5ms. These latencies translate to SSD-level performance of: random read 4KB IOPS up to 1M, random write 4KB IOPS up to 400K, sequential read up to 7GB/s, sequential write up to 6GB/s (depending on controller and over-provisioning). The center X-DEC design reduces RC delay, improving access times compared to edge-decoded architectures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect SSD-level performance with sub-100µs read latency and competitive program/erase times.

faqs.question: Does X4-9070 support advanced features like ONFI EZ-NAND?
faqs.answer: Yes, the X4-9070 supports advanced ONFI features: (1) EZ-NAND - simplifies controller design by handling complex NAND management internally; (2) Volume addressing - enables flexible die selection and management; (3) Multi-plane operations - allows parallel program/erase in multiple planes for higher throughput; (4) Read cache - enables pipelined read operations for improved performance; (5) Program cache - allows pipelined program operations. These features enable SSD controllers to achieve optimal performance and reliability. Contact LiTong FAE for detailed feature support and implementation guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: X4-9070 supports modern ONFI features for simplified controller design and optimal performance.

faqs.question: What is the data retention specification for X4-9070?
faqs.answer: The X4-9070 data retention specifications: (1) Standard retention - 10 years at 40°C for fresh data; (2) End-of-life retention - 1 year at 40°C after endurance cycling; (3) Temperature acceleration - retention decreases with higher temperatures, following Arrhenius behavior; (4) Read disturb - minimal impact with proper wear leveling and read scrubbing. For enterprise applications, YMTC recommends periodic read scrubbing and maintaining spare blocks for data refresh. The 232-layer technology with improved cell structure provides robust data retention characteristics competitive with industry standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: 10-year retention at 40°C for fresh data; implement read scrubbing for long-term data integrity.

faqs.question: Is X4-9070 available in automotive grade?
faqs.answer: Yes, the X4-9070 is available in AEC-Q100 qualified automotive grade: (1) Temperature grade - available in Grade 2 (-40°C to +105°C) and Grade 3 (-40°C to +85°C); (2) Enhanced testing - additional screening for automotive reliability requirements; (3) Traceability - full lot traceability for automotive quality systems; (4) PPAP support - complete documentation for automotive approval. Automotive grade X4-9070 is suitable for infotainment systems, ADAS storage, digital clusters, and autonomous driving platforms. Contact LiTong for automotive grade availability and qualification documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Automotive grade X4-9070 available with AEC-Q100 qualification for vehicle applications.

products.name: 192-Layer TLC 3D NAND Flash
products.shortDescription: Fourth-generation TLC 3D NAND with 192 layers, Xtacking 3.0 architecture, offering high density up to 512Gb per die.
descriptionParagraphs: The X3-9070 is YMTC's fourth-generation TLC 3D NAND flash memory, featuring 192 layers and the proven Xtacking 3.0 architecture. It delivers excellent performance and reliability for mainstream storage applications.
Built on Xtacking 3.0, the X3-9070 achieves up to 512Gb storage capacity per die with 1200MT/s I/O speed. The architecture enables high-density storage with competitive cost per GB.
The X3-9070 is ideal for mainstream enterprise SSDs, consumer storage, and mobile applications requiring balanced performance and cost. It offers 4,000 P/E cycle endurance for typical workloads.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Storage Solutions
faeReview.content: The X3-9070 hits the sweet spot for mainstream storage applications. In my experience with consumer and enterprise SSD designs, the 192-layer technology provides excellent cost-performance ratio. The 1200MT/s interface is well-supported by most modern controllers, making integration straightforward. I particularly like the flexibility in die density options - 256Gb for cost-optimized designs and 512Gb for higher capacity. The 4,000 P/E cycle endurance is sufficient for most client and read-intensive enterprise workloads. For applications that don't need the absolute latest technology, the X3-9070 offers significant cost savings compared to 232-layer alternatives while still delivering excellent performance. It's my go-to recommendation for mainstream SSD designs.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 192-layer TLC NAND ideal for mainstream enterprise and consumer SSDs

alternativeParts.reason: Latest generation with highest performance and density
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring maximum performance and capacity

alternativeParts.reason: Comparable performance with better cost structure

companionParts.description: Consumer SATA SSD based on X3-9070 NAND

faqs.question: What is the price difference between X3-9070 and X4-9070?
faqs.answer: The X3-9070 is positioned as a cost-optimized solution with approximately 15-20% lower cost per GB compared to X4-9070. The exact pricing depends on volume, package type, and market conditions. While X4-9070 offers higher performance (1600MT/s vs 1200MT/s) and density (1Tb vs 512Gb), the X3-9070 delivers excellent value for mainstream applications where absolute maximum performance is not required. For high-volume consumer SSD applications, the X3-9070 offers compelling cost-performance ratio. Contact LiTong sales for specific pricing based on your volume requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose X3-9070 for cost-sensitive mainstream applications; choose X4-9070 for premium performance.

faqs.question: Can X3-9070 be used for enterprise applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the X3-9070 is suitable for many enterprise applications: (1) Read-intensive workloads - 4,000 P/E cycles sufficient for databases, analytics, and content delivery; (2) Boot drives - excellent performance for server boot applications; (3) Cache tiers - high-speed read performance for caching; (4) Mainstream storage - cost-effective capacity for general-purpose storage. For write-intensive workloads (databases, virtualization), consider X4-9070 with higher endurance. The X3-9070's 1200MT/s interface enables enterprise-class SSD performance up to 6GB/s sequential read when paired with capable controllers.
faqs.decisionGuide: X3-9070 suitable for read-intensive and mainstream enterprise; use X4-9070 for write-intensive workloads.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of X3-9070?
faqs.answer: The X3-9070 features optimized power consumption: (1) Active read - approximately 100-120mW per die at maximum throughput; (2) Active program - approximately 150-180mW per die during write operations; (3) Standby - less than 10mW per die in standby mode; (4) Deep power down - less than 1mW per die. These power levels enable efficient SSD designs with good battery life for mobile applications and low operating costs for data centers. The Xtacking 3.0 architecture reduces power consumption compared to conventional NAND designs through optimized peripheral circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low power consumption makes X3-9070 suitable for mobile and battery-powered applications.

faqs.question: Does X3-9070 support industrial temperature range?
faqs.answer: Yes, the X3-9070 is available in industrial temperature grade: (1) Temperature range - -40°C to +85°C operating temperature; (2) Enhanced screening - additional testing for industrial reliability requirements; (3) Extended qualification - longer burn-in and stress testing; (4) Long-term supply - guaranteed availability for industrial product lifecycles. Industrial grade X3-9070 is suitable for industrial automation, networking equipment, telecommunications infrastructure, and other harsh environment applications. Contact LiTong for industrial grade availability and specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Industrial grade X3-9070 available for harsh environment applications requiring -40°C to +85°C operation.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for X3-9070?
faqs.answer: X3-9070 lead times vary based on configuration and volume: (1) Standard products - typically 6-8 weeks for common configurations; (2) High-volume orders - 8-10 weeks for large quantities; (3) Custom configurations - 10-12 weeks for special requirements; (4) Samples - 2-4 weeks for engineering samples. LiTong maintains strategic inventory for high-demand configurations to support urgent requirements. For production programs, we recommend forecasting and buffer stock arrangements to ensure continuous supply. Contact LiTong sales for specific lead times based on your requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard lead time 6-8 weeks; contact LiTong for urgent requirements and volume pricing.

categories.name: QLC 3D NAND Flash
categories.description: YMTC QLC 3D NAND flash memory stores 4 bits per cell, offering highest storage density and lowest cost per GB for read-intensive applications.
categories.longDescription: YMTC QLC 3D NAND flash memory stores 4 bits per cell, offering highest storage density and lowest cost per GB for read-intensive applications. Built on advanced Xtacking architecture, QLC NAND delivers up to 33% higher density than TLC at significantly lower cost. As an authorized YMTC distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance for QLC NAND products. QLC is ideal for cold storage, content delivery networks, and read-heavy workloads where write endurance requirements are moderate. YMTC QLC products feature 1,000+ P/E cycle endurance and are available in standard BGA packages for SSD applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select YMTC QLC NAND
selectionGuide.description: Guide to selecting QLC NAND for read-intensive storage applications

series.name: X4-9050 Series
series.description: High-density QLC NAND for read-intensive applications

faqs.question: What is QLC NAND and when should I use it?
faqs.answer: QLC (Quad-Level Cell) NAND stores 4 bits per cell using 16 voltage levels. It offers the highest density and lowest cost per GB but with lower endurance than TLC. Use QLC when: (1) Your workload is primarily read-heavy; (2) You need maximum capacity at lowest cost; (3) Write endurance requirements are moderate (1,000+ P/E cycles); (4) Applications include cold storage, CDNs, and archival. QLC is not recommended for write-intensive applications like databases or boot drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose QLC for read-intensive applications requiring maximum capacity at lowest cost.

faqs.question: How does QLC compare to TLC in terms of performance?
faqs.answer: QLC vs TLC comparison: (1) Density - QLC offers 33% higher density (4 bits vs 3 bits per cell); (2) Cost - QLC has lower cost per GB; (3) Endurance - QLC has 1,000+ P/E cycles vs 3,000-5,000 for TLC; (4) Write performance - QLC has slower write speeds due to more precise voltage programming; (5) Read performance - comparable read speeds for both. QLC is ideal for read-heavy workloads while TLC is better for mixed workloads. Choose based on your specific application requirements and workload characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: QLC offers higher density and lower cost; TLC offers better endurance and write performance.

products.name: 232-Layer QLC 3D NAND Flash
products.shortDescription: High-density QLC NAND with 232 layers, offering 1.33Tb per die for maximum capacity applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The X4-9050 is YMTC's high-density QLC 3D NAND flash memory, featuring 232 layers and Xtacking 4.0 architecture. It delivers maximum storage density for cost-sensitive, read-intensive applications.
With 4 bits per cell, the X4-9050 achieves up to 1.33Tb storage capacity per die. This enables SSDs with unprecedented capacity at competitive price points.
The X4-9050 is ideal for cold storage, content delivery networks, and read-heavy enterprise applications where capacity and cost are primary concerns.

faeReview.author: Sarah Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Cloud Storage
faeReview.content: The X4-9050 QLC NAND is perfect for our cloud storage customers who need massive capacity at competitive prices. In CDN and cold storage applications, the read-heavy workload profile matches QLC strengths perfectly. We're seeing customers deploy 16TB+ SSDs using this NAND. The key is understanding your workload - if reads dominate and writes are infrequent, QLC offers unbeatable value. I recommend implementing proper wear leveling and over-provisioning to maximize QLC endurance. For write-heavy applications, stick with TLC, but for the right use cases, QLC is a game-changer for cost optimization.
faeReview.highlight: High-density QLC NAND ideal for cold storage and read-intensive applications

companionParts.description: TLC NAND for mixed workload applications

faqs.question: Is QLC reliable enough for enterprise use?
faqs.answer: Yes, QLC is reliable for appropriate enterprise applications: (1) Endurance - 1,000+ P/E cycles sufficient for read-heavy workloads; (2) Error correction - modern LDPC ECC handles QLC error rates effectively; (3) Data retention - 10-year specification for fresh data; (4) Field proven - deployed in cloud storage and CDNs. Best practices: implement strong ECC (80+ bits/1KB), use adequate over-provisioning (15-20%), monitor wear levels, and plan for workload-appropriate endurance. QLC is NOT recommended for write-intensive databases or boot drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: QLC is reliable for read-heavy enterprise applications with proper ECC and monitoring.

categories.name: Enterprise SSD Solutions
categories.description: Complete enterprise SSD solutions based on YMTC NAND, featuring high performance, high capacity, and enterprise-grade reliability for data center applications.
categories.longDescription: Complete enterprise SSD solutions based on YMTC NAND, featuring high performance, high capacity, and enterprise-grade reliability for data center applications. As an authorized YMTC distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive support for enterprise SSD designs including reference designs, firmware integration, and qualification assistance. Our enterprise solutions leverage YMTC X4-9070 NAND to deliver up to 8TB capacity with PCIe Gen4 performance. Solutions include NVMe and SATA interfaces, power-loss protection, and end-to-end data protection. Ideal for database acceleration, virtualization, cloud storage, and high-performance computing applications.

selectionGuide.title: Enterprise SSD Solution Selection
selectionGuide.description: Guide to selecting the right enterprise SSD solution

series.name: PC411 Series
series.description: High-performance NVMe SSD for enterprise applications

faqs.question: What makes an SSD enterprise-grade?
faqs.answer: Enterprise SSDs differ from consumer SSDs in several key areas: (1) Endurance - higher P/E cycles and DWPD ratings; (2) Power-loss protection - capacitors to ensure data integrity; (3) End-to-end data protection - ECC and CRC throughout data path; (4) Consistent performance - sustained performance under heavy loads; (5) Reliability - higher MTBF and UBER specifications; (6) Features - hot-plug support, SMBus, thermal throttling. YMTC-based enterprise SSDs from LiTong include all these features for mission-critical data center applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose enterprise SSDs for data center applications requiring high reliability and consistent performance.

products.name: Enterprise NVMe SSD
products.shortDescription: High-performance enterprise NVMe SSD based on X4-9070 NAND, delivering up to 7GB/s read speed.
descriptionParagraphs: The PC411 is a high-performance enterprise NVMe SSD based on YMTC X4-9070 NAND. It delivers exceptional performance for demanding data center applications.
With PCIe Gen4 x4 interface, the PC411 achieves up to 7,000 MB/s sequential read and 1M IOPS random read performance. Available in capacities from 1TB to 8TB.
Enterprise features include power-loss protection, end-to-end data protection, and 1-3 DWPD endurance rating. Ideal for database acceleration and virtualization.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Enterprise Storage
faeReview.content: The PC411 enterprise SSD showcases YMTC NAND's capability in demanding data center environments. We've deployed these in several cloud provider environments with excellent results. The 7GB/s read performance matches premium enterprise SSDs, while the YMTC NAND provides cost advantages. Power-loss protection works reliably - we've tested with thousands of power cycles without data corruption. The 1M IOPS random read performance is particularly impressive for database workloads. For customers considering YMTC-based enterprise storage, the PC411 is an excellent starting point with proven reliability.
faeReview.highlight: Enterprise NVMe SSD with proven reliability in data center deployments

companionParts.description: NAND flash for custom SSD designs

faqs.question: What is DWPD and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: DWPD (Drive Writes Per Day) measures how many times you can write the full drive capacity daily over the warranty period. For example, 1 DWPD on a 4TB drive means you can write 4TB per day for 5 years. Enterprise SSDs typically offer 1-3 DWPD vs 0.3-0.5 for consumer SSDs. Higher DWPD means: (1) Better suitability for write-heavy workloads; (2) Longer lifespan in demanding applications; (3) Higher cost but better TCO for intensive use. Choose DWPD based on your workload: 1 DWPD for general use, 3 DWPD for databases or virtualization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 1 DWPD for general enterprise use; 3 DWPD for write-intensive applications.

categories.name: Embedded Storage Solutions
categories.description: Embedded storage solutions including eMMC and UFS based on YMTC NAND for mobile, IoT, and automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: Embedded storage solutions including eMMC and UFS based on YMTC NAND for mobile, IoT, and automotive applications. As an authorized YMTC distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive support for embedded storage designs. Our eMMC 5.1 and UFS 2.2/3.1 solutions offer low power consumption, small form factors, and high reliability for space-constrained applications. YMTC NAND-based embedded storage is ideal for smartphones, tablets, automotive infotainment, industrial IoT devices, and smart home applications. Solutions include industrial and automotive temperature grades with AEC-Q100 qualification available.

selectionGuide.title: Embedded Storage Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide to selecting eMMC and UFS solutions

series.name: PE300 Series
series.description: eMMC 5.1 solutions for mobile and embedded

faqs.question: Should I choose eMMC or UFS for my application?
faqs.answer: Choose between eMMC and UFS based on performance needs: (1) eMMC - up to 400MB/s, lower cost, mature ecosystem, good for entry/mid-range devices; (2) UFS - up to 2,320MB/s, higher performance, better for flagship smartphones and automotive. eMMC is suitable for: IoT devices, entry-level smartphones, industrial HMI. UFS is better for: flagship smartphones, automotive ADAS, high-performance tablets. UFS has higher power efficiency per MB/s transferred. Consider your performance requirements, cost budget, and target market when selecting.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose eMMC for cost-sensitive applications; UFS for high-performance requirements.

products.name: eMMC 5.1 Embedded Storage
products.shortDescription: Industrial grade eMMC 5.1 solution based on X3-9070 NAND for embedded applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The PE321 is an industrial grade eMMC 5.1 embedded storage solution based on YMTC X3-9070 NAND. It provides reliable storage for industrial and automotive applications.
With HS400 interface, the PE321 delivers up to 400MB/s transfer speed. Available in capacities from 8GB to 128GB with industrial temperature range support.
Features include enhanced power-loss protection, bad block management, and wear leveling. AEC-Q100 Grade 3 qualification available for automotive applications.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Embedded Systems
faeReview.content: The PE321 eMMC has been reliable in our industrial customer deployments. The industrial temperature range (-40°C to +85°C) handles harsh environments well. We've seen consistent performance across temperature extremes. The power-loss protection is robust - critical for industrial applications where unexpected power events are common. For automotive customers, the AEC-Q100 qualification path is straightforward. The YMTC NAND inside provides good cost structure compared to alternatives. I recommend this for any industrial or automotive embedded application requiring reliable storage.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable industrial eMMC with wide temperature range and automotive qualification

comparison.Power: Higher > Lower per MB/s (UFS more efficient)

faqs.question: What is the difference between commercial and industrial grade eMMC?
faqs.answer: Commercial vs industrial grade eMMC differences: (1) Temperature range - commercial: 0°C to +70°C, industrial: -40°C to +85°C; (2) Testing - industrial grade undergoes additional screening and burn-in; (3) Reliability - industrial grade has tighter quality control; (4) Supply - industrial grade has longer lifecycle commitment; (5) Cost - industrial grade is typically 20-30% more expensive. Choose industrial grade for: outdoor applications, automotive, industrial automation, medical devices. Commercial grade is sufficient for: consumer electronics, office equipment, indoor applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose industrial grade for harsh environments; commercial grade for standard consumer applications.
